issues.description: The Session 2 safety gate for `dabblerSessionSets.setupVerification` is too narrow: it only blocks when `activity-log.json` contains a recognized `verification_mode` record, and it silently treats unreadable logs as 'no record'. Locked decision D3 requires the B→A reverse to stop being offered once any activity-log record exists, and the seed rewrite should not proceed when the durable history cannot be inspected. As written, a not-started-but-already-touched set can still be switched, creating the spec-vs-history drift D3 is meant to prevent.
issues.recommendation: Add a separate guard that fail-loud inspects `activity-log.json` for any existing entries (not just `verification_mode` ones), and refuse the rewrite if the file cannot be read/parsed or if any record is present. Keep `verificationModeRecordExists` only as the Python-parity helper from step 1, not as the sole Session 2 command gate.
issues.verificationMethod: Code review of setupVerification.ts's guard against the locked D3 language ('B→A is never offered once any activity-log record exists') and the spec Session 2 step 3 wording ('no activity-log record exists').
issues.resolution_notes: Added inspectActivityLog to verificationModeRewrite.ts (no-records | has-records | unreadable; ANY entry counts as history; shapeless/unparseable reads as unreadable). setupVerification now refuses the rewrite with an error on unreadable logs (fail-loud) and with an info message when any history exists; verificationModeRecordExists kept only as the Python-parity helper. 4 new gate tests; suite 835 passing.
